options.text: If

Both A) If and B) whether are correct.whether is more commonly used when the sentence presents alternatives (whether or not). So, the preferred answer is:

options.text: Whether

questions.text: They said, "Would that she were alive."
questions.subject: English

options.text: They wished that she were alive.

options.text: They wished that she had been live.

options.text: They wish that she were alive.

options.text: They wished that she had been alive.

questions.explanation: In converting direct speech to indirect speech, especially with expressions of wish, it is important to change the tense to reflect the hypothetical nature of what is being expressed. In this case, 'would that she were alive' is a subjunctive expression indicating a wish about the past. The correct indirect form is 'They wished that she had been alive,' using the past perfect tense to indicate an unreal past situation. Other options fail to use the correct tense or form, either keeping the subjunctive 'were' or using the wrong verb form 'live.'
